source_intake_record.raw_question_text: You are doing exercises on a Nautilus machine in a gym to strengthen your deltoid (shoulder) muscles. Your arms are raised vertically and can pivot around the shoulder joint. The deltoid muscle is attached to the humerus \( 15.0\, \text{cm} \) from the shoulder joint If you have set the tension in the cable of the machine to \( 36.0\, \text{N} \) on each arm, what is the tension in each deltoid muscle if you simply hold your outstretched arms in place?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual is likely essential rather than optional
The question describes a biomechanics/torque setup ('pivot around the shoulder joint', muscle attachment point, cable tension) that commonly requires a diagram for force directions and moment arms
A single registered image exists and registry_passed=true, so there is an available visual asset to support the problem
multi_step: Solving likely requires combining multiple facts: cable tension, shoulder pivot, deltoid attachment distance, and geometry from the visual
This is a statics/torque equilibrium problem rather than direct lookup, implying at least two reasoning steps
Multiple-choice format with close numeric distractors suggests a computed result from a multi-step setup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a concise numeric answer ('605N') is easy to compare against a derived result
choices provide explicit candidate values, which supports objective checking
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true indicate the sample is structurally complete for downstream verification

possible rounding/unit-format variation for the final numeric answer
